mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE declines by > 0.2pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank rate hikes that increase discount rates for equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDP growth combined with low federal funds rates creates a favorable environment for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 > 1% and FEDFUNDS < 2% fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during supply-side disruptions reduces corporate output capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by > 2% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains above 100k signal robust labor market demand that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts above 1.2 million units indicate strong demand for residential construction, boosting equity markets through sectoral momentum and positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1200.

mechanism_narrative: High real sales (RSAFS) combined with low unemployment creates a positive feedback loop between consumer spending and labor market strength, generating significant S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of RSAFS > 480k and UNRATE < 4% fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates during a tightening cycle increase borrowing costs for corporations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.5pp.
